course: Introduction to Advanced Concepts in Java

modules.name: File I/O and Design Patterns
modules.description: File I/O and Design Patterns — 8 lessons.

lessons.title: File I/O
topics: File I/O Packages and Streams/Readers/Writers Terminology
Creating a File and Choosing Absolute vs. Relative Paths
Writing to a File with PrintWriter and try-with-resources
Appending and Reading Files with FileWriter and BufferedReader
Deleting Files, java.nio.file.Files, and Directories

lessons.title: Application Layers
topics: Chunking and the Concept of a Software Layer
The Three-Layer Architecture — User Interface, Domain, and Data Access
Layer Relationships and Dependency Direction
Models vs. Layers — a Shared Vocabulary Across Layers

lessons.title: The Repository Pattern
topics: The Repository Pattern and the Problem It Solves
DAO, CRUD, and Where the Repository Fits
A Layer-Specific Exception Type
Defining the Repository Interface
Implementing the File-Backed Repository: CRUD Methods
Private Helper Methods and the Public/Private Boundary

lessons.title: Testing the Repository
topics: Why File I/O Tests Need a Known Good State
JUnit 5 Setup and Building the Known Good State with @BeforeEach
Arrange, Act, Assert: Testing the "R" in CRUD
Testing Create and Update Operations
Testing Delete and Confirming Full CRUD Coverage

lessons.title: The Domain Layer
topics: Where Domain Rules Belong: Initial, Evolved, and Ideal Enforcement Scenarios
The Result Type: Reporting Success, Errors, and the Affected Model
The Service Class: Dependency Injection and Pass-Through Methods
Validating Input and Delegating to the Repository: add, update, deleteById

lessons.title: Testing the Service
topics: Known Good State: Testing a Service vs. Testing a Repository
Test Doubles: Mocks, Stubs, and Fakes
Building `MemoryRepositoryDouble`: An In-Memory Test Double
Dependency Injection and Testing Read/Create/Update Operations
How Much Testing Is Enough? Diminishing Returns

lessons.title: The MVC Pattern
topics: MVC Roles: Model, View, and Controller
Inversion of Control and Sequence Diagrams
Building the User Interface Layer: ConsoleIO, TextIO, and View
The Controller: Coordinating View and Service

lessons.title: Dependency Injection
topics: What Dependency Injection Is: Loose Coupling vs. Tight Coupling
Constructor Injection
Setter Injection
Wiring the Complete Application: The App Entry Point and the Dependency Chain

lessons.title: BigDecimal
topics: Floating-Point Imprecision and Why Financial Code Needs BigDecimal
Creating a BigDecimal Instance
BigDecimal Arithmetic: Immutable Operations
Scale, setScale, and RoundingMode
Rounding During Division and divideAndRemainder

lessons.title: Temporal Types in SQL
topics: Session Date Format and SQL Server's Temporal Data Types
Formatting Dates with FORMAT()
Working with Parts of Dates
Calculating with Dates: MIN, MAX, and DATEDIFF
objects: Object: Lesson: Temporal Types in SQL
Assessment: Quiz: Temporal Types in SQL

lessons.title: Temporal Types in Java
topics: Java's Core Temporal Types
Formatting Dates and Times with DateTimeFormatter
Extracting Date Parts and Building Dates from Parts
Date Calculations: Comparisons and Differences
objects: Object: Lesson: Temporal Types in Java
Assessment: Quiz: Temporal Types in Java

lessons.title: Spring
topics: Scope, Adoption, and Governance of Spring
The Four Core Spring Projects: Framework, Data, Security, and Boot
From Hand-Wired Dependencies to Spring's ApplicationContext
Why Spring DI Matters: Run-Time Configuration and a Foundational Technology

lessons.title: Spring DI with XML Configuration
topics: Adding Spring DI to a Maven Project and Registering a Bean with XML
Constructor Injection with Value and Reference Arguments; Resolving Ambiguity with id
Injecting Collections and Optional Dependencies with Setter Injection
Singleton vs. Prototype Bean Scope
objects: Object: Lesson: Spring DI with XML Configuration
Assessment: Quiz: Spring DI with XML Configuration

lessons.title: Three-Layer Architecture DI with Annotations
topics: AnnotationConfigApplicationContext and @ComponentScan
Registering Components Backward: @Component for Controller, View, and ConsoleIO
@Autowired: Constructor, Setter, and Field Injection
@Service and @Repository for MemoryService and MemoryFileRepository
Supplying Primitive/String Values with @Value
External Configuration with @PropertySource and Comparing XML vs Annotations
objects: Object: Lesson: Three-Layer Architecture DI with Annotations
Assessment: Quiz: Three-Layer Architecture DI with Annotations

lessons.title: Lambdas
topics: The Repetition Problem and Generalizing with a Filter Method
Lambda Syntax: From Named Methods to Concise Expressions
Functional Interfaces: Where Lambdas Are Valid
Method References: Type::methodName and instance::methodName

lessons.title: Streams
topics: Stream<T> Fundamentals: Creating Streams and Navigating the API
Filtering Streams: filter, skip, and limit
Terminal Operations: forEach, findFirst, and collect
Sorting with Comparator and Transforming with map/flatMap
Aggregate Operations: count, summarizingInt, joining, anyMatch/allMatch, and groupingBy
Problem-Solving Strategy: Filter-Early, Sort-Late
